title: Proclamation No. 658, s. 1940
text:
MALACAÑAN PALACE MANILA

PROCLAMATION NO. 658

RESERVING FOR MILITARY PURPOSES A PARCEL OF THE PUBLIC DOMAIN SITUATED IN THE BARRIO OF LIBERTAD, MUNICIPALITY OF BUTUAN, PROVINCE OF AGUSAN, ISLAND OF MINDANAO.

Upon the recommendation of the Secretary of Agriculture and Commerce and pursuant to the provisions of section eighty-three of Commonwealth Act Numbered One hundred and forty-one, as amended, I hereby withdraw from sale or settlement and reserve for military purposes, under the administration of the Chief of Staff, Philippine Army, subject to private rights, if any there be, and for future delimitation and survey, and to the conditions that the forest existing on the area should be managed and disposed of in accordance with the Forest and Internal Revenue Laws and Regulations, and that as soon as the area is no longer needed for military purposes, the same shall revert to the category and as part of the public forest and turned over to the Bureau of Forestry for administration and management, the following parcel of the public domain, situated in the barrio of Libertad, municipality of Butuan, Province of Agusan, Island of Mindanao, and particularly described in Bureau of Forestry sketch, Agusan P. M. D. No. 1075, to wit:

Beginning at a point marked “1” on plan, being S. 37° W., about 1,940 m. from barrio Libertad, Butuan, Agusan, thence S. 40° E., 380 m. to point “2”; thence following Malalag Creek upstream general S.W., about 275 m. to point “3”; thence S. 13° W., 420 m. to point “4”; thence S. 12° W., 280 m. to point “5”; thence S. 10° W., 320 m. to point “6”; thence S. 14°. 280 m. to point “7”; thence S. 10° W., 270 m. to point “8”; thence S. 13° W., 210 m. to point hence S. 12° W., 270 m. to point “10”; thence S. 22° W., 290 m. to point “11”; thence S. 18° W., 340 m. to point “12″; thence S. 10″ W., 240 m. to point “13”; thence S. 10° W., 360 m. to point “14”; thence S. 10° W., 250 m. to point “15”: thence S. 10° W., 350 m. to point “16”; thence following Bancasi Creek downstream general NE. about 340 m. to point “86”; thence N. 34° E., 420 m. to point “87”; thence N. 35° E., 810 m. to point “88”; thence S. 80° E., 640 m. to point “89”; thence S. 65° E., 280 m. to point “1”, point of beginning.

Containing an area of 2,028 hectares.

Point 1, narra 60 cm.; point 2, cumagascas 30 cm.; point 3. narra 55 cm.; point 4, T. pantay 30 cm.; point 5, itaman 30 cm.; point 6, mango 30 cm.; point 7, W. lauan 60 cm., point 8, W. lauan 30 cm.; point 67, Palaquium sp. 50 cm.; point 68, Is-is 40 cm.; point 69, lanutan 30 cm.; point 70, a point; point 71, pili 30 cm.; point 72, W. lauan 30 cm.; point 73, W. lauan 40 cm.; point 74, cagamcam 40 cm.; point 75, dalindingan 30 cm.; point 76, mayapis 60 cm.; point 77, apitong 40 cm.; point 78, W. lauan 50 cm.; point 79, Palaquium sp. 40 cm.; point 80, guijo 40 cm.; point 81, yacal 80 cm.; point 82, akle 40 cm.; point 83, anilao 40 cm.; point 84, tanglin 40 cm.; point 85, tangile 15 cm.; point 86, magtalisay 40 cm.; point 87, narra 90 cm.; point 88, balobo 25 cm.; point 89, W. lauan 65 cm.

Bounded on the northeast by Agusan project No. 22; on the east, by Agusan project No. 22 and public forest; on the south, by public forest; on the west, by public forest; and on the northwest, by Agusan project No. 22.

In witness whereof, I have hereunto set my hand and caused the seal of the Commonwealth, of the Philippines to be affixed.

Done at the City of Manila, this twenty-third day of December, in the year of Our Lord, nineteen hundred and forty, and of the Commonwealth of the Philippines, the sixth.

MANUEL L. QUEZON President of the Philippines

By the President: JORGE B. VARGAS Secretary to the President

Source: Presidential Museum and Library
